claim: Induction of urokinase receptor signaling in podocytes has no effect on foot process effacement and proteinuria.

result.claim: Induction of urokinase receptor signaling in podocytes has no effect on foot process effacement and proteinuria.
result.reasoning:
The claim is refuted by evidence indicating that induction of urokinase receptor signaling in podocytes *does* have an effect on podocyte injury, including proteinuria and focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS), which involves foot process effacement.

Specifically, paper ID 4a7ad9b8091fb0edf1a60ef989319f86913932eb, an animal study with a credibility rating of 2.5/5, directly contradicts the claim. Its abstract states that "Plaur-transgenic mice manifested podocyte injury, including c-Src phosphorylation, proteinuria, and focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS)." This clearly shows that increased urokinase receptor (uPAR) signaling (via Plaur RNA expression) leads to proteinuria and FSGS, which is characterized by foot process effacement.

Furthermore, paper ID 40517817, while an abstract with a lower credibility rating (1.0/5), supports the idea that uPAR signaling is involved in podocyte dysfunction. It mentions that "pharmacologic targeting of the uPAR-FPR axis protects podocytes from damage and may preserve renal function in primary podocytopathies," implying that uPAR signaling contributes to podocyte damage, which would include foot process effacement and proteinuria.

evidence.text: PAI-1 interacts with the urokinase receptor (uPAR).
evidence.source: Plasminogen Activator Inhibitor-1 in Chronic Kidney Disease

evidence.text: The Mol Cancer Ther publication (2013) was about a urokinase receptor-derived peptide inhibiting VEGF-dependent directional migration and vascular sprouting.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: Down-regulation of podoplanin in NHEKs via transfection with podoplanin siRNA inhibited their migration.
evidence.source: Platelets Regulate the Migration of Keratinocytes via Podoplanin/CLEC-2 Signaling during Cutaneous Wound Healing in Mice.

evidence.text: PAI-1−/− podocytes synthesize less collagen in response to AngII than wild-type cells.
evidence.source: Plasminogen Activator Inhibitor-1 in Chronic Kidney Disease

evidence.text: Pharmacologic targeting of the uPAR-FPR axis may preserve renal function in primary podocytopathies.
evidence.source: Potential targeting of urokinase-type plasminogen activator receptor-formyl peptide receptor signaling to prevent recurrence in posttransplant primary podocytopathies.

evidence.text: The research paper by Resnati M, Pallavicini I, Wang JM, Oppenheim J, Serhan CN, Romano M, Blasi F focused on the fibrinolytic receptor for urokinase activating the G protein-coupled chemotactic receptor FPRL1/LXA4R.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: GlnK uridylylation appears not to be required for nifHDK gene regulation in vivo.
evidence.source: Molecular Origins of Transcriptional Heterogeneity in Diazotrophic Klebsiella oxytoca.

evidence.text: Insulin resistance induces podocyte injury.
evidence.source: Insulin resistance surrogates predict major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and chronic kidney disease: a retrospective cohort study.

evidence.text: Cimini et al. (2019) demonstrated that inhibition of the interaction between podoplanin-expressing cells and podoplanin-binding cells reduce inflammation post MI.
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: CAR-T cells can inhibit podoplanin/CLEC-2 binding.
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: Renal biopsies from subjects with FSGS manifested increased c-Src phosphorylation in podocytes.
evidence.source: c-Src is in the effector pathway linking uPAR and podocyte injury.

evidence.text: The Plaur-transgenic mice manifested podocyte injury, including c-Src phosphorylation, proteinuria, and focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).
evidence.source: c-Src is in the effector pathway linking uPAR and podocyte injury.

evidence.text: The only known podoplanin receptor is C-type lectin-like receptor 2 (CLEC-2).
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: uPAR can interact with formyl peptide receptors (FPRs) in podocytes.
evidence.source: Potential targeting of urokinase-type plasminogen activator receptor-formyl peptide receptor signaling to prevent recurrence in posttransplant primary podocytopathies.

evidence.text: Syk-dependent signaling activation through podoplanin can lead to the induction of innate immune genes in myeloid cells.
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: Insulin resistance can induce podocyte injury.
evidence.source: Insulin resistance surrogates predict major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and chronic kidney disease: a retrospective cohort study.

evidence.text: The study investigated the involvement of A20 signaling alterations in podocytes in the development of kidney injury.
evidence.source: Podocyte A20/TNFAIP3 Controls Glomerulonephritis Severity via the Regulation of Inflammatory Responses and Effects on the Cytoskeleton

evidence.text: Increased expression of the urokinase-type plasminogen activator receptor (uPAR) has been associated with podocyte dysfunction in primary podocytopathies.
evidence.source: Potential targeting of urokinase-type plasminogen activator receptor-formyl peptide receptor signaling to prevent recurrence in posttransplant primary podocytopathies.

evidence.text: Inhibition of the interaction between podoplanin-expressing cells and podoplanin-binding cells reduces inflammation post-MI (Cimini et al., 2019).
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: Each glomerulus relays information from a specific type of receptor.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: The secretion of active ILEI requires mobilizing its intracellular protein pool in a urokinase-type plasminogen activator receptor (uPAR)-dependent manner
evidence.source: The FAM3C locus that encodes interleukin-like EMT inducer (ILEI) is frequently co-amplified in MET-amplified cancers and contributes to invasiveness.

evidence.text: Ultrastructural analysis of A20 podocyte-knockout mouse glomeruli revealed podocytopenia associated with foot process effacement, karyopyknosis, micronuclei, and podocyte detachment.
evidence.source: Podocyte A20/TNFAIP3 Controls Glomerulonephritis Severity via the Regulation of Inflammatory Responses and Effects on the Cytoskeleton
